name: Etouffee
recipeIngredient: 1 jalapeno (seeded, if desired, for less heat), finely chopped
1 green bell pepper, stemmed, seeded & chopped
4 cups cooked basmati or long-grain white rice
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the tofu into 1/2-inch cubes.
2. Arrange the tofu pieces on a baking sheet lined with three layers of paper towels. Cover with another three layers and place a second baking sheet on top. Compress the tofu for 30 minutes using heavy cans or books.
3. Preheat the oven to 300 degrees F.
4. Coat the bottom of an oven-proof, 12-inch skillet with nonstick cooking spray (or use an oven-proof nonstick skillet). Heat 2 tablespoons of oil in the skillet over medium heat.
5. Add the tofu and sauté until lightly browned on all sides. Transfer the tofu to drain on paper towels.
6. Reduce heat to medium-low and add the remaining oil to the pan. Stir in the flour and cook, stirring constantly, for about 5 minutes until bubbling.
7. Place the pan in the oven and roast the mixture for 20 minutes longer, stirring well every 5 minutes. Remove the pan from the oven and place over medium heat.
8. Add the onion, scallions, jalapeno, bell peppers, celery, garlic, black pepper, cayenne, and thyme. Cook, stirring frequently, until the vegetables are soft, about 10 minutes.
9. Increase the heat to high and add the tomatoes. Cook, stirring until bubbling, then add the stock, Worcestershire sauce, and salt. Cook, stirring until the mixture thickens.
10. Reduce heat to low and stir in the tofu. Simmer, partially covered, for 45 minutes.
11. Stir in the parsley and taste for seasoning. To serve, spoon the rice onto serving plates and ladle the etouffee over it.
